growth_records.strain: EN76 (soil ammonia-oxidizing archaeon; not explicitly named in Methods)

up.method: not reported; primary energy substrate, medium 2 mM NH4Cl, specific rate not quantified in paper
not reported; medium 1 mM pyruvate (organic carbon supplement), specific rate not quantified
not reported; autotrophic carbon source supplied via gassing (2% CO2), rate not quantified
not reported; consumed in ammonia oxidation, rate not quantified

sec.method: not reported; nitrite is the product of archaeal ammonia oxidation, specific production rate not quantified for N. viennensis chemostat

growth_records.cite: Hodgskiss et al. Unexpected complexity of the ammonia monooxygenase in archaea. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01367-3. [PMC10030591]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrososphaera viennensis was grown as a continuous culture in 2 L bioreactors (Eppendorf) filled with 1.5 L of freshwater medium (FWM) with modified trace element solution, 7.5 uM FeNaEDTA, 2 mM NH4Cl, and 1 mM pyruvate at 42 C and pH 7.5.
growth_records.notes: Continuous (chemostat) culture; at steady state mu = dilution rate D. Paper states applied dilution rates ranged 0.035-0.07 h-1; this record captures the low endpoint D=0.035 h-1. mu = D = 0.035 h-1. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.035 = 19.80 h. No specific uptake/secretion (qS, qO2, qNO2) rates or biomass yields are reported, so those fields are null (compound identities retained: NH4+ substrate, pyruvate + CO2 carbon, O2, and NO2- product). FWM full composition not given in main text (cited to refs 37,38); components explicitly stated are listed.

growth_records.cite: Hodgskiss et al. Unexpected complexity of the ammonia monooxygenase in archaea. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01367-3. [PMC10030591]
growth_records.snippet: The applied dilution rates ranged from 0.035 to 0.07 h-1. [N. viennensis grown as continuous culture in 1.5 L FWM with 7.5 uM FeNaEDTA, 2 mM NH4Cl, 1 mM pyruvate at 42 C and pH 7.5; gassed 98% air / 2% CO2].
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: mu = dilution rate D at steady state. This record captures the high endpoint of the stated applied dilution rate range (0.035-0.07 h-1), i.e. D=0.07 h-1. mu = D = 0.07 h-1. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.07 = 9.90 h. No specific substrate/product fluxes or biomass yields reported; uptake/secretion compound identities retained with null rates. FWM full composition cited to external refs; only explicitly stated components listed.

growth_records.medium: Freshwater medium for N. viennensis (carbon-replete standard condition A); detailed composition in Supplemental Methods

up.method: thermodynamic model stoichiometry; experimentally 0.06 mol C/mol N

growth_records.cite: Hodgskiss et al. Metabolic response of a chemolithoautotrophic archaeon to carbon limitation. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00732-25. [PMC12542715]
growth_records.snippet: predicted a carbon consumption of 0.064 mol of inorganic carbon per 1 mol of ammonia ... 1.411 O2 + 0.064 CO2 + NH3 -> 0.953 H2O + 0.017 C3.85H6.69O1.78N + 0.983 NO2- + 0.983 H+
growth_records.notes: Growth conditions: 42 C, pH ~7.2, closed-system batch, 2 mM ammonia, varying bicarbonate (standard A = 2 mM). Values here are MODELED stoichiometric coefficients from the thermodynamic growth equation (per 1 mol NH3), NOT measured specific fluxes and with no time base -> cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Model predicts 0.064 mol inorganic C / mol NH3 (=0.128 mol C / 2 mol NH3); experimental value used was 0.06 mol C/mol N. Biomass formula C3.85H6.69O1.78N at 0.017 mol/mol NH3. No measured growth rate or doubling time is reported in the extractable main text (growth followed via nitrite-production curves in Fig 1; full medium in Supplemental Methods, not in XML) -> growth_rate null, not fabricated. One representative record for standard condition A; conditions B-G differ only in carbon (0-0.1 mM) and ROS scavenger (catalase vs pyruvate).

growth_records.strain: N. viennensis EN76 (AOA, Thaumarchaeota group I.1b, soil)
growth_records.medium: Freshwater medium (FWM) amended with trace elements, vitamins and Fe-NaEDTA

up.method: Michaelis-Menten fit; Km(app)=3.3-6.8 uM total ammonium (range for two Nitrososphaera isolates, n=4 each); protein-normalized Vmax not reported in text

sec.method: AOA incomplete oxidizer; product nitrite; rate not protein-normalized in text

yields.Yxs: 304.3 ug protein per mol NH3 oxidized (s.d. 17.9, n=4)

growth_records.cite: Kits et al. Kinetic analysis of a complete nitrifier reveals an oligotrophic lifestyle. Nature (2017). DOI:10.1038/nature23679. [PMC5600814]
growth_records.snippet: N. viennensis was cultured in a freshwater medium (FWM) amended with ... sodium bicarbonate and sodium pyruvate ... 2 mM and 1 mM ... pH was adjusted to 7.6 ... that of N. viennensis was 304.3 ug protein per mol NH3 (s.d. 17.9, n = 4).
growth_records.notes: Km(app, total ammonium)=3.3-6.8 uM (range shared with gargensis, n=4 each). Molar growth yield 304.3 ug protein/mol NH3 (reported). FWM base salts referenced (ref 13/33), only amendments enumerated: 2 mM NaHCO3, 1 mM pyruvate, HEPES buffer, 1 mM NH4+ working, plus 1 ml/l each trace elements/vitamins/Fe-NaEDTA; full base composition not given -> medium_composition_stated false. pH 7.6 (stated). Optimum temperature 42 C. No growth rate/doubling time measured.
